features.featureTitle: Create a login landing page (inspired by https://edumanage-9ftdqajs.manus.space/login/teacher) that presents four role-based login entry points: Student, Teacher, Institute, and Parents. Each role card/button should navigate to a dedicated login route (e.g., /login/student, /login/teacher, /login/institute, /login/parent). The visual design should include a role selector with distinct icons and role-appropriate accent colors consistent with the existing per-role design tokens already defined in index.css.

Implement per-role login pages at /login/student, /login/teacher, /login/institute, and /login/parent. Each page shows a login form (username/password fields with a Submit button — no backend authentication required, use mock/static credentials or simply a single 'Enter as [Role]' button). On successful login, store the active role in React state or context and redirect to the main app.

After login, render only the menu and dashboard that belongs to the authenticated role. Student login → show only the Student dashboard and its sub-navigation (Academic Roadmaps, Exam Hub, Goals, Teams, Marketplace, Feedback). Teacher login → show only the Faculty Hub and its sub-navigation. Institute login → show only the Institute Command Center and its sub-navigation. Parents login → show only the Parent Insight Portal and its sub-navigation. All other role tabs must be hidden from the Navbar.

Add a role-aware auth context (e.g., AuthContext) that stores the currently logged-in role ('student' | 'teacher' | 'institute' | 'parent' | null). This context should be consumed by the Navbar to conditionally render tabs, and by the App router to guard routes — unauthenticated users are redirected to the login landing page.
